skill.name: p-video
skill.description: Generate videos with Pruna P-Video and WAN models via inference.sh CLI. Models: P-Video, WAN-T2V, WAN-I2V. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, audio support, 720p/1080p, fast inference. Pruna optimizes models for speed without quality loss. Triggers: pruna video, p-video, pruna ai video, fast video generation, optimized video, wan t2v, wan i2v, economic video generation, cheap video generation, pruna text to video, pruna image to video
skill.summary: Generate videos using Pruna-optimized AI models (P-Video, WAN-T2V, WAN-I2V) via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill providing usage examples for the inference.sh CLI video generation tools. The static analyzer flagged 25 external_command patterns and 8 network patterns, but these are all false positives: the 'external_commands' are markdown code blocks showing CLI usage (not actual execution), and the 'network' patterns are documentation URLs and example placeholders. No malicious code detected.

content.user_title: Generate AI Videos with Pruna P-Video
content.value_statement: Create high-quality videos from text prompts or images using optimized AI models. Pruna's optimized P-Video and WAN models deliver fast inference without quality loss.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using P-Video model
Create videos from images using WAN-I2V model
Add audio to videos with P-Video audio support
Use draft mode for faster, cheaper video previews
List and browse available Pruna video models
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account
Video generation costs apply per second or per video
Maximum duration depends on model (typically 5-10 seconds)

use_cases.title: Content Creator Video Production
use_cases.description: Quickly generate video clips for social media content, YouTube intros, or marketing materials using text prompts.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators, social media managers, marketers

use_cases.title: Prototype Visualization
use_cases.description: Create draft videos to visualize concepts or storyboards before committing to full production.

use_cases.title: AI-Assisted Video Workflows
use_cases.description: Integrate AI video generation into automated content pipelines for scalable video production.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second video at 720p showing [INSERT PROMPT describing the scene you want]
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a simple video from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-Video Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate my image at [INSERT IMAGE URL] with gentle camera movement and natural motion. Use prompt: [INSERT MOTION DESCRIPTION]
prompt_templates.scenario: Bringing static images to life with AI

prompt_templates.title: High-Resolution Cinematic Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 1080p cinematic quality video with the following prompt: [INSERT SCENE DESCRIPTION]. Duration: [INSERT SECONDS], Style: [INSERT STYLE]
prompt_templates.scenario: Producing high-quality video for professional use

prompt_templates.title: Budget-Conscious Draft Preview
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a draft-quality video for quick review with prompt: [INSERT PROMPT]. Use draft mode for fast, inexpensive preview.

output_examples.input: infsh app run pruna/p-video --input '{"prompt": "drone shot flying over a forest at sunset", "duration": 5, "resolution": "720p"}'
output_examples.output: A 5-second 720p video showing an aerial drone perspective of a forest during golden hour sunset

output_examples.input: infsh app run pruna/wan-i2v --input '{"prompt": "gentle movement, clouds drifting", "image": "https://example.com/landscape.jpg", "resolution": "720p"}'
output_examples.output: A 720p animated version of the landscape image with subtle cloud movement and natural motion

output_examples.input: infsh app run pruna/p-video --input '{"prompt": "person talking in interview", "audio": "https://example.com/narration.mp3"}'
output_examples.output: A video of a person speaking with audio synchronized from the provided MP3 file

best_practices: Use specific, detailed prompts describing camera angle, lighting, and motion for better results
Start with draft mode to test prompts quickly before generating high-quality output
Combine image-to-video with audio for engaging narrated content
anti_patterns: Using overly vague prompts like 'make a cool video' without specific details
Generating maximum duration videos without testing with shorter drafts first
Ignoring resolution requirements - 1080p costs more than 720p

faq.question: What is Pruna P-Video?
faq.answer: Pruna P-Video is an optimized video generation model that creates videos from text prompts or images. It uses Pruna's optimization technology for faster inference without quality loss.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the inference.sh CLI (infsh). Install it with: npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools

faq.question: How much does video generation cost?
faq.answer: Costs vary by model and resolution. WAN models start at $0.05 per video for 480p. P-Video pricing is per second and varies by resolution and draft mode.

faq.question: What is draft mode?
faq.answer: Draft mode generates lower-quality previews faster and cheaper. Use it to test your prompts before generating the final high-quality video.

faq.question: Can I add audio to my videos?
faq.answer: Yes, P-Video supports audio input. You can provide an MP3 URL and the model will sync the video with your audio.

faq.question: What is the difference between WAN-T2V and WAN-I2V?
faq.answer: WAN-T2V is for text-to-video generation. WAN-I2V is for image-to-video, animating static images into videos with motion.
